recent_coach_change: Yes - major recent change. Otto Addo was dismissed in March 2026 and replaced by veteran Portuguese coach Carlos Queiroz, appointed on 14 April 2026, just two months before the World Cup.
base_formation: Built around high intensity, quick transitions and vertical attacks; Queiroz typically favours a compact, organised structure (4-3-3 / 4-2-3-1)
qualification_path: Won CAF Group I with 25 points (8 wins, 1 draw, 1 defeat), finishing ahead of Madagascar and Mali. Sealed qualification on 12 October 2025 with a 1-0 home win over Comoros, Mohammed Kudus scoring the decisive goal.
key_players: Antoine Semenyo (Bournemouth) — primary attacking threat following Kudus' absence; pace, power and one-v-one ability
Thomas Partey (free agent/veteran) — experienced midfield anchor headlining the squad
Jordan Ayew (forward) — experienced attacker and senior figure
Lawrence Ati-Zigi (goalkeeper) — first-choice keeper, started the Wales friendly

injuries_doubts.issue: Quadriceps muscle injury suffered in January with the Tottenham; complications in recovery ruled him out of the tournament entirely
Centre-back sidelined by injury, missed the cut

squad_notes: Queiroz named his final 26-man squad after trimming a 28-man pre-tournament camp in Cardiff, Wales. Star attacker Mohammed Kudus was ruled out injured. Former captain Andre Ayew missed the cut, ending what would have been his fourth World Cup. Centre-back Alexander Djiku also out injured. Antoine Semenyo and Thomas Partey headline the squad.
warmup_assessment: Underwhelming. Ghana drew 1-1 with Wales in Cardiff on 2 June 2026, conceding a stoppage-time equaliser to substitute Lewis Koumas after Caleb Yirenkyi had put them ahead, before flying to Washington to continue preparations.
shape_verdict: Ghana qualified comfortably but arrive destabilised by a March coaching change and the loss of talisman Kudus to injury; a late equaliser conceded against Wales underlined lingering defensive fragility under new boss Queiroz.

news.headline: Carlos Queiroz appointed Ghana head coach for the 2026 World Cup, replacing the dismissed Otto Addo
Mohammed Kudus ruled out of the 2026 World Cup with a quadriceps injury
Wales 1-1 Ghana in pre-World Cup friendly; Black Stars concede late equaliser
